input.background: The ANOVA in Exercises 13.3 found that birth weight of four groups of infants differed significantly according to the smoking status of mothers (P=0.042). Table 13.3 lists the data for the analysis. Data are also available online in the file SMOK_BW.SAV.
input.question: Conduct post hoc comparisons using the LSD method. Summarize these results in concise narrative form.

output.answer:
Least squared difference (LSD) tests

| Null hypothesis | x̄i-x̄j | SE | P-value (2-sided) |
|-----------------|---------|------|-----------------|
| H0: μ1-μ2=0 | 0.0857 | 0.67848 | 0.901 |
| H0: μ1-μ3=0 | 1.1191 | 0.64690 | 0.098 |
| H0: μ1-μ4=0 | 1.6129 | 0.60512 | 0.014 |
| H0: μ2-μ3=0 | 1.0334 | 0.67848 | 0.143 |
| H0: μ2-μ4=0 | 1.5272 | 0.63876 | 0.026 |
| H0: μ3-μ4=0 | 0.4938 | 0.60512 | 0.424 |

Calculated by SPSS (Rel. 11.0.1. 2001. Chicago: SPSS Inc.)

Summary in concise narrative form: Differences between group 1 and group 4 and between group 2 and group 4 are statistically significant. The difference between group 1 and group 3 is marginally significant.

birth_weight.value: Birth weights of infants (values not explicitly provided in the problem statement, but assumed to be numerical data from Table 13.3 or the SMOK_BW.SAV file)
birth_weight.description: The birth weight of infants, which is the dependent variable being analyzed.

smoking_status.value: Smoking status of mothers (categorical groups, e.g., non-smoker, light smoker, moderate smoker, heavy smoker, as implied by the problem statement)
smoking_status.description: The smoking status of mothers, which is the independent variable used to group the infants for comparison.

meta_info.caption: Smoking and birth weight, post hoc comparisons.
